plugins.name: ask-questions-if-underspecified
plugins.description: Clarify ambiguous requirements by asking questions before implementing. Only when invoked explicitly.

plugins.name: git-cleanup
plugins.description: Safely analyzes and cleans up local git branches and worktrees by categorizing them as merged, squash-merged, superseded, or active work.

plugins.name: differential-review
plugins.description: Security-focused differential review of code changes with git history analysis and blast radius estimation

plugins.name: modern-python
plugins.description: Modern Python best practices. Use when creating new Python projects, and writing Python scripts, or migrating existing projects from legacy tools.

plugins.name: variant-analysis
plugins.description: Find similar vulnerabilities and bugs across codebases using pattern-based analysis

plugins.name: semgrep-rule-creator
plugins.description: Create custom Semgrep rules for detecting bug patterns and security vulnerabilities

plugins.name: insecure-defaults
plugins.description: Detects insecure default configurations including hardcoded credentials, fallback secrets, weak authentication defaults, and dangerous values in production

plugins.name: devcontainer-setup
plugins.description: Create pre-configured devcontainers with Claude Code and language-specific tooling

plugins.name: workflow-skill-design
plugins.description: Teaches design patterns for workflow-based Claude Code skills and provides a review agent for auditing existing skills

plugins.name: yt-transcript
plugins.description: Fetch YouTube video transcripts and save them as clean Markdown notes.

plugins.name: taskmanager-agent
plugins.description: Backend-agnostic task management agent. Pulls issues from your task tracker (Linear, etc.), creates execution plans, works through them via git worktrees and PRs, and updates statuses. Use /tm-health to set up, /tm-work-backlog to process the backlog, /tm-assign to work a specific issue.

plugins.name: grill-me
plugins.description: Interview the user relentlessly about a plan or design until reaching shared understanding, resolving each branch of the decision tree. Use when user wants to stress-test a plan, get grilled on their design, or mentions "grill me".
